lede: A look at April 18 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ski Mask the Slump God and Edgar Wright, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2026 Kyiv shooting
events.desc: Seven people are killed and fourteen others are injured in a mass shooting in Kyiv, Ukraine

events.title: John Demjanjuk
events.desc: In Israel John Demjanjuk is sentenced to death for war crimes committed in World War II, although the verdict is later overturned.

events.title: The Republic of Ireland Act 1948
events.desc: The Republic of Ireland Act comes into force, declaring Éire to be a republic and severing Ireland's "association" with the Commonwealth of Nations.

events.title: Operation Big Bang
events.desc: The Operation Big Bang, the largest non-nuclear man-made explosion to that time, destroys bunkers and military installations on the North Sea island of Heligoland, Germany.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Doolittle Raid on Japan: Tokyo, Yokohama, Kobe and Nagoya are bombed.

events.title: Superman
events.desc: Superman debuts in Action Comics #1 (cover dated June 1938).

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: French pilot Roland Garros is shot down and glides to a landing on the German side of the lines.

events.title: Joan of Arc
events.desc: Joan of Arc is beatified in Rome.

events.title: The Spirits Book
events.desc: The Spirits Book by Allan Kardec is published, marking the birth of Spiritualism in France.

events.title: Royal Academy of History
events.desc: Real Academia de la Historia ("Royal Academy of History") is founded in Madrid.

births.name: Joseph L. Goldstein
births.line: American biochemist and geneticist, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Samuel P. Huntington
births.line: American political scientist, author, and academic (died 2008)

births.name: James McCune Smith
births.line: African-American physician, apothecary, abolitionist, and author (died 1865)

deaths.text: Dickey Betts, American guitarist, singer, songwriter and composer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Cyril VI of Constantinople (Eastern Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Blessed Marie of the Incarnation
